name: Praying Mantis
description: The only known invertebrate with stereoscopic 3D vision, using binocular disparity for precise depth perception during prey strikes. Raptorial forelegs execute strikes in 50-70 milliseconds — among the fastest predatory movements in the insect world. Head rotation of 180 degrees provides a wide surveillance field from a stationary ambush position. Masters patience as strategy: stillness as computation, waiting as optimization.

core_attributes.neurology: stereoscopic vision, rapid strike motor circuits
core_attributes.pattern: 3D binocular vision, ambush patience, ultra-fast raptorial strike
